description: The inherent value or dignity of something; worth that doesn't depend on external attribution. 'Rolia' names what beings have simply by existing—dignity not earned but intrinsic.
sound_symbolism: The word resonates with dignity. 'Ro' grounds with solid presence, worth inherent. 'Li' lifts with recognized quality. Then 'a' opens to acceptance of this truth.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'theli miona rolia phelu.' (Each person holds worth.) Related to but distinct from 'sone' (value), which is attributed; rolia is inherent dignity.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Inherent worth—all beings possess dignity by virtue of sentience.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Recognizing worth—communication that honors the dignity of all participants.
